summary: Restyle reader shell to match Viggo's Obsidian setup
description: Make Mega Vault Viewer visually follow Viggo's Obsidian setup from the screenshot: open dark reader canvas, compact left sidebar/chrome, extra views as small top icons, and frontmatter/links/item/outline/calendar as click-open popovers instead of always-open heavy panels.
acceptance: Two-column layout with no permanent right rail; document canvas is open/dark and not card-heavy; item/frontmatter/links/outline/calendar are reachable from compact top icons; frontmatter remains click-open; visual style uses Obsidian-like compact dark chrome, magenta headings and cyan links; tests/build/browser smoke pass.
verification: npm --workspace apps/desktop run test;npm --workspace apps/desktop run build;git diff --check;browser smoke at http://127.0.0.1:1420 has no console errors

evidence.summary: Original prompt check: mega vault viewer restyled toward Viggo's Obsidian screenshot; implemented open dark two-column shell, compact left/sidebar chrome, no permanent right rail, extra views moved to top icon popovers, frontmatter remains click-open. Follow-up materialization: no separate follow-up task created; remaining richer proof would require a Tauri runtime/document-open smoke, but web smoke + TS/build covers current change. Micro devil / anti-slop: result avoids card-heavy SaaS panels and keeps reader canvas open; icon popovers have aria labels; risk is web smoke cannot exercise Tauri invoke/opened-document state. Recheck: reran npm --workspace apps/desktop run test, npm --workspace apps/desktop run build, git diff --check, browser smoke on http://127.0.0.1:1421 with zero console errors; killed dev servers and stray node listeners. Self-reflect: process miss was reporting local changes before committing/pushing, causing visibility confusion; fixed now by materializing as .go task and proceeding to commit/push, no skill mutation needed because go-now already requires commit/push.
